October 2025
Negative Review Deep Dive — October 2025

October 2025 saw 5,243 negative reviews (1–2 stars). The dominant complaint pattern centered on account bans and suspensions (16.5%), with users reporting permanent bans without clear reasons and an opaque appeals process. Technical issues ranked second (13.7%), including login failures and the app failing to open. Crashes, bugs, and performance degradation (11.4%) were widespread—audio continuing to play after closing the app was a particularly viral complaint (top review: 2,303 upvotes). Users also expressed growing frustration with UI changes from updates (9.4%) and broken sound / upload functionality (8.1%).

Ban / Suspension / Account Issues 16.5%

Permanent bans without explanation, "community guidelines" violations with no details, frozen accounts, failed appeals. Top phrases: "account banned", "community guidelines", "unfreeze account".

Search / Login / Technical 13.7%

App won't open, login failures, screens stuck on loading, search returning irrelevant results. Many users forced to uninstall/reinstall repeatedly.

Crashing / Bugs / Glitches 11.4%

Audio bleeding after app close was the signature bug. Freezing during live streams, videos stuck/repeating, lag after recent updates. Phrases: "keeps crashing", "black screen", "keeps playing".

Update / UI Changes 9.4%

Disliked layout changes, removed features, scroll behavior regressions. Users wanted to revert to previous versions. Buttons relocated or removed entirely.

Sound / Camera / Upload Issues 8.1%

Failed uploads, audio desync, sound overlapping, filters/effects broken after update. Creators unable to post content for hours.

Content / Algorithm / FYP 7.6%

Shadowbanning complaints, FYP showing repetitive/irrelevant content, creators seeing 0 views. Algorithm perceived as punishing small creators.

Representative Complaints (Most Upvoted)

“I’m so tired of the music from an ad continuously playing after I close the app. I end up having to force stop it. Now today I didn’t even have the app open and music just started blasting from my phone.”
2★ · 887 upvotes
“The ‘You’re adding favorites too fast!’ thing is absolutely unbearable. It never lasts the same amount of time, and even when it goes away, it comes back after five or so videos.”
2★ · 2,303 upvotes
“Anytime I’m invited up to a box, the app locks up then crashes. This has happened like 4 or 5 times now. It has cost me growth.”
2★ · 485 upvotes
“I follow someone, and TikTok immediately removed the follow, and takes them out of the list of who I’m following. I can’t follow anyone for more than a few seconds.”
1★ · 449 upvotes
January 2026
Negative Review Deep Dive — January 2026

January 2026 produced 6,874 negative reviews—a 31% increase over October 2025. This spike coincided with TikTok’s ownership transition to US management. The most dramatic shifts were in FYP / algorithm complaints (7.6% → 14.3%, nearly doubling) as creators reported 0-view posts, and technical / login failures surging to 18.2%. Complaints about ads and monetization jumped 2 percentage points, with TikTok Shop ads dominating the frustration. Censorship and free speech concerns emerged as a new major theme, with 197 mentions of “censorship” and 30+ mentions of “free speech.” The word “internet connection” became the #1 bigram, suggesting widespread connectivity or server-side issues.

Search / Login / Technical 18.2%

Login failures surged—“keeps saying try later” and “internet connection” errors even on stable WiFi. Users unable to log back into accounts, forced to delete & reinstall. Top phrase: "internet connection" (65 mentions).

Ban / Suspension / Account 16.7%

Permanent bans shown before even logging in on fresh installs. Account deletion requests ignored. Top highly-upvoted review (4,717 votes) described instant ban on new download. Phrases: "terms service", "delete account".

Crashing / Bugs / Glitches 14.8%

App described as “slow and clunky” post-transition. Stories not posting, reposts not showing, profile features missing (STEM tab, shop tab). Crash frequency increased notably.

Content / Algorithm / FYP 14.3%

Biggest growth category (+6.7pp). Creators reporting 0-view posts despite good content and followers. FYP “broken for this long”—top review got 49,630 upvotes. Algorithm perceived as suppressing reach. Phrase: "fyp" appeared 236 times in negative reviews alone.

Update / UI Changes 13.4%

Comment section forced to light mode in dark mode app. Layout regressions, features vanishing after updates. Unwanted AI summarization on lives. Users requesting rollback. "uninstalled reinstalled" (28 mentions).

Privacy / Safety / Censorship 8.3%

Emerged as new major concern. “Censorship” (197 mentions), “free speech” / “freedom speech” rhetoric spiked. Users linked content suppression to new US ownership. Privacy policy and terms-of-service changes cited.

Ads / Monetization 6.5%

TikTok Shop ads dominating FYP. “Every other thing is an ad, or an undisclosed ad.” Users feeling the platform became commercialized. Ad count increased noticeably versus prior months.

Sound / Camera / Upload 7.9%

Music library restricted (“no music to work with”), upload failures persisted, profile picture updates broken, editing tools degraded.

Representative Complaints (Most Upvoted)

“Videos are getting 0 views and not going to the For You page after upload. Even with good content and followers, reach is completely blocked. This seems like an algorithm or system bug.”
1★ · 49,630 upvotes
“So many TikTok shop ads on my FYP. Stop treating me like an idiot baby with this new automatic AI summarization on lives. Nobody likes that feature.”
1★ · 13,060 upvotes
“The FYP is broken, I can’t choose my preferences, can’t update my profile picture, my posts ain’t going through, no music to work with and I can’t clear my cache.”
2★ · 9,612 upvotes
“The app is completely trash now that it has been taken over by the US. It crashes ALL THE TIME and my STEM tab and TikTok Shop tab is completely gone.”
1★ · 5,446 upvotes
“It keeps showing account permanently banned, and I haven’t even logged in to any account. I downloaded it new and that’s the first thing showing.”
1★ · 4,717 upvotes
Comparative Analysis — Oct 2025 vs Jan 2026

Negative review volume grew 31% from October to January. The table below shows how complaint categories shifted, with the biggest escalations in algorithm/FYP issues, login/technical failures, and update dissatisfaction.

Complaint Theme Oct 2025 Jan 2026 Change
Content / Algorithm / FYP 7.6% 14.3% +6.7pp
Search / Login / Technical 13.7% 18.2% +4.5pp
Update / UI Changes 9.4% 13.4% +4.0pp
Crashing / Bugs / Glitches 11.4% 14.8% +3.4pp
Privacy / Safety / Censorship 5.6% 8.3% +2.6pp
Ads / Monetization 4.5% 6.5% +2.0pp
Ban / Suspension / Account 16.5% 16.7% +0.1pp
Sound / Camera / Upload 8.1% 7.9% −0.3pp
Notifications / Messages 5.9% 5.7% −0.2pp

Key Takeaways

1
FYP / algorithm breakdown is the #1 escalating pain point. The proportion of negative reviews mentioning FYP, algorithm, or reach issues nearly doubled from Oct to Jan. The single most-upvoted review in the entire dataset (49,630 votes) is about 0-view posts. This signals a potentially systemic reach/distribution issue post-transition.
2
Technical stability degraded significantly. Login failures, crashes, and bugs collectively jumped from ~36% to ~48% of all negative reviews. The “internet connection” error became the top complaint phrase, suggesting server-side instability rather than user-side issues.
3
Censorship and ownership concerns emerged. “Censorship” had 197 mentions in Jan alone (near-zero in Oct). Users explicitly linked content suppression, terms-of-service changes, and privacy policy updates to the US ownership transition. “Free speech” became a recurring phrase.
4
Ad fatigue intensified. TikTok Shop ad complaints grew +2pp. Users described the FYP as dominated by ads, with undisclosed sponsored content eroding trust. This is compounding the FYP dissatisfaction.
5
Account bans remain persistently high. At ~16.5% in both months, unexplained bans are a chronic issue unaffected by the transition. The opaque appeals process continues to generate the most emotional reviews.
6
Unwanted AI features are a new friction point. Automatic AI summarization on lives, forced location prompts, and AI-generated content labels were cited in high-upvote January reviews as intrusive features users cannot disable.
Generated on 2026-03-20 18:28:28 · Data: Google Play Store · App: TikTok (com.zhiliaoapp.musically) · Reviews analyzed: 98,610
